page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% tier. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing also show weak to poor performance. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average-tier results in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth remains consistently in the average tier for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks, though the overall Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.r5.3xlarge is its modest single-threaded compute capability compared to its generous memory allocation. It offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high memory capacity but do not demand intensive, high-frequency single-core processing. This resource density makes it suitable for memory-bound workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing, where the 96.0 GB of RAM can be fully utilized. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core execution—such as bottom 25% Geekbench single-core and poor single-threaded gzip compression—and average multi-core capabilities, including average-tier stress-ng multi-core and Redis SET operations. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound applications that do not require high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, showing average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized KVM instance with 12 Intel Xeon cores and 96 GB RAM, suitable for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring 96 GB of RAM and 12 dedicated cores for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
